title: For whom do we regenerate? A Capability-oriented and justice-based framework for evaluating urban regeneration metrics in PNRR-funded Urban Integrated Plans. Protocol for a scoping and systematic thematic review.
author: Fabiani, Maria
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description: This article presents the protocol for a mixed methods scoping and systematic thematic review that aims to critically examine the evaluation metrics used in urban regeneration projects. The research combines Amartya Sen and Martha Nussbaum’s Capability Approach with Nancy Fraser’s tripartite theory of justice (redistribution, recognition, and representation) to map, classify, and assess the extent to which current evaluation systems reflect or neglect principles of spatial justice and individual and collective agency. The outcome of the review is a transformative framework for designing situated, equity-oriented metrics. The article outlines the theoretical foundations, methodological strategy, and steps for future implementation of the framework within real-world governance settings, with a specific focus on the Metropolitan City of Rome and Urban Integrated Plans (PUI) funded through Italy’s National Recovery and Resilience Plan (PNRR).
